title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Transport Domain Separation
summary: RSS and Atom transport structs leak into storage, missing data is invented, HTML and text are conflated, source identifiers are discarded, and updates cannot be explained. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go transport domain separation increment in loss-aware normalized feed and entry model. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Convert format-specific transport values into validated feed, entry, person, link, category, content, and provenance domain types.
Classify malformed, oversized, too-deep, unsupported-encoding, entity, cancellation, and trailing-document failures with bounded diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Transport Domain Separation · Theory Feed Go Transport Doma…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4703 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.

checks.description: The decision applies Feed Go Transport Domain Separation to the changed stakeholder case.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
